description: HP 8664A synthesized signal generator (0.1 MHz to 3 GHz). Programmed over HP-IB (IEEE-488.2) using HP-SL (Hewlett-Packard System Language), an SCPI-like hierarchical command language. Commands have a short form (uppercase letters) and long form (uppercase + lowercase); case is ignored. Keywords within a statement are separated by colons, statements by semicolons. Command messages must be terminated by a linefeed (ASCII 10) or EOI. Optional keywords appear in [square brackets]; a leading [:LEVel], [:DEPTh], etc. may be omitted. ON|OFF and 1|0 are interchangeable for switch parameters; queries return 1 or 0. MINimum/MAXimum/UP/DOWN may be used as parameters. Most settings have set and query (?) forms. Same command set as the 8665A and 8665B (to 4.2/6 GHz). Pulse modulation requires Option 008.

identity.description: *IDN? returns four comma-separated fields: HEWLETT-PACKARD, model number (e.g. 8664A), serial number in HP format (or 0 if unknown), and firmware revision. Example: HEWLETT-PACKARD,8664A,2833A00873,REV 1.0.0

commands.name: identify
commands.description: Identification query. Returns four comma-separated fields: 'HEWLETT-PACKARD', model number, serial number (HP format or 0 if unknown), firmware revision.

commands.name: reset
commands.description: Reset command. Performs an instrument preset, setting all operating parameters to their known *RST states. Does not affect status reporting, error/message queues, or the 50 storage registers. Must be sent on its own line.

commands.name: clear_status
commands.description: Clear status command. Clears the Status Byte and associated status data structures (Standard Event Status Register, all event registers) and clears the output and error queues.

commands.name: event_status_enable
commands.description: Standard Event Status Enable Register set/query.

commands.name: event_status_register
commands.description: Queries the Standard Event Status Register.

commands.name: service_request_enable
commands.description: Service Request Enable Register set/query.

commands.name: status_byte
commands.description: Read Status Byte query. Returns the HP-IB Status Byte.

commands.name: operation_complete
commands.description: Operation complete command. Sets the OPC bit in the Standard Event Status Register when a sweep or learn operation is complete.

commands.name: wait
commands.description: Wait-to-continue command. Prevents further input/output processing until all commands in the message containing *WAI have completed.

commands.name: self_calibration
commands.description: Self-calibration query. Performs an internal self-calibration and returns an integer error code (0 = no failures). Equivalent to Special Function 171 / CALibration[:ALL]?.

commands.name: self_test
commands.description: Self-test query. Performs internal instrument-level diagnostics and returns an integer error code (0 = no failures). Equivalent to Special Function 170.

commands.name: options
commands.description: Option query. Returns mnemonics of reportable installed options separated by commas, or 0 if none. E.g. GAAS PULSE = Option 008 (pulse modulation); COMM_DISCR = Option 004 (low noise mode).

commands.name: save_state
commands.description: Saves the instrument state in the specified storage register. 50 registers available: registers 0-9 store all front-panel settings, registers 10-49 store only frequency and amplitude.

commands.name: recall_state
commands.description: Recalls the instrument state stored in the specified register. Registers 0-9 hold all front-panel settings, 10-49 hold only frequency and amplitude.

commands.name: frequency_cw
commands.description: Set/query the non-swept (CW) RF output frequency. Does not disable sweep. *RST value is 1500 MHz.

parameters.name: frequency
parameters.range: 0.1 MHz to 3000 MHz (8664A); UP|DOWN|MIN|MAX accepted; default unit Hz

commands.name: frequency_step
commands.description: Set/query the step size used by UP/DOWN for frequency commands (CW, STARt, STOP, CENTer, SPAN, MARKer). MIN/MAX refer to smallest/largest programmable step size. *RST value is 10 MHz.

commands.name: frequency_start
commands.description: Set/query the sweep start frequency. Does not enable sweep. Coupled to STOP/CENTer/SPAN. *RST value is 100 kHz (10 kHz on serial prefix 3015A and above).

commands.name: frequency_stop
commands.description: Set/query the sweep stop frequency. Does not enable sweep. Coupled to STARt/CENTer/SPAN. *RST value is the maximum frequency (3 GHz on 8664A).

commands.name: frequency_center
commands.description: Set/query the sweep center frequency. Does not enable sweep. Coupled to STARt/STOP/SPAN. *RST value is (STARt+STOP)/2.

commands.name: frequency_span
commands.description: Set/query the sweep frequency span. Does not enable sweep. Coupled to STARt/STOP/CENTer. *RST value is STOP-STARt.

commands.name: frequency_manual
commands.description: Controls the frequency during a manual sweep. Limited to FREQuency:STARt..FREQuency:STOP. *RST value equals FREQuency:STARt.

commands.name: frequency_offset
commands.description: Sets a reference frequency offset added to entered/displayed absolute frequencies (CW, STARt, STOP; not FM or SPAN). Changes displayed values only, not RF output. Displayed = (Hardware Freq x Multiplier) + Offset. *RST value is 0 Hz.

commands.name: frequency_multiplier
commands.description: Sets a reference multiplier applied to entered/displayed frequencies (CW, STARt, STOP, FM, SPAN). Changes displayed values only, not RF output. Resolution is integer or 1/integer. Equivalent to Special Function 111. *RST value is 1.

commands.name: frequency_synthesis
commands.description: Sets the synthesis mode (equivalent to a front-panel MODE SELECT key). Setting this turns FREQuency:SYNThesis:AUTO OFF.

commands.name: frequency_synthesis_auto
commands.description: When ON, firmware automatically selects the synthesis mode (MODE SELECT AUTO). When OFF, the current synthesis mode is held. *RST value is ON.

commands.name: frequency_mode
commands.description: Selects which commands control the frequency subsystem. SWEep enables FREQ:STARt/STOP/CENTer/SPAN/MANual control. *RST value is CW.

commands.name: frequency_instantaneous
commands.description: Returns the instantaneous RF output frequency during DIGitized FM. Equivalent to Special Function 121.

commands.name: amplitude_level
commands.description: Set/query the CW output amplitude (LEVel is assumed if omitted). POWer is an alias for AMPLitude. AMPLitude:SOURce refers to source EMF rather than output. *RST value is -140.0 dBm.

parameters.name: amplitude
parameters.range: UP|DOWN|MIN|MAX accepted; default unit is AMPLitude:UNIT

commands.name: amplitude_step
commands.description: Set/query the amplitude step size used by UP/DOWN. Sending a UNITs suffix sets AMPLitude:STEP:UNIT accordingly. *RST value is 10 dB.

commands.name: amplitude_step_unit
commands.description: Set/query the unit for amplitude steps (V or dB). If V, AMPLitude UP/DOWN steps in volts regardless of AMPLitude:UNIT; if dB, steps in dB. *RST value is dB.

commands.name: amplitude_state
commands.description: Turns the RF output ON or OFF. OFF disables the output; setting LEVel does not turn it ON. *RST value is OFF.

commands.name: amplitude_unit
commands.description: Specifies the implied unit for all power/amplitude parameters (except when AMPLitude:STEP:UNIT is used) and the unit used in query responses. *RST value is dBm.

commands.name: amplitude_upper_limit
commands.description: Sets the maximum upper limit for AMPLitude (Special Function 103). If set below current AMPLitude, AMPLitude is reduced and an error issued. MIN is 1 dB above the minimum allowable amplitude. *RST value is 19.9 dBm.

commands.name: amplitude_attenuation
commands.description: Set/read the attenuator value in dB (Special Function 101). Setting attenuation sets POW:ATT:AUTO OFF and changes output level. *RST value depends on option configuration.

commands.name: amplitude_attenuation_auto
commands.description: When ON, firmware controls the attenuators; OFF holds the attenuator range at its present setting (Special Function 100). *RST value is ON.

commands.name: amplitude_gain
commands.description: Adjusts the displayed/entered power level without changing actual output level. *RST value is 0 dB.

commands.name: amplitude_alc_bandwidth_auto
commands.description: Enables/disables automatic ALC bandwidth selection based on frequency and modulation; OFF forces widest bandwidth (Special Function 104: off=narrowband, on=wideband). *RST value is ON.

commands.name: amplitude_alc_muting
commands.description: ALC muting (Special Function 105).

commands.name: calibration_all
commands.description: Performs an instrument self-calibration and returns an error code (0 = no failures). Alias to *CAL?.

commands.name: calibration_amplitude_state
commands.description: Enables/disables use of AMPLitude correction data (Special Function 102). *RST value is ON.

commands.name: am_depth
commands.description: Set/query AM depth in percent. *RST value is 0%.

commands.name: am_depth_step
commands.description: Set/query AM depth step size in percent (used by UP/DOWN). *RST value is 1%.

commands.name: am_state
commands.description: Turns AM modulation ON or OFF. Setting AM:DEPth does not turn AM ON. *RST value is OFF.

commands.name: am_source
commands.description: Selects AM source: INTernal or EXTernal. *RST value is INTernal.

commands.name: am_coupling
commands.description: Set/query source coupling for AM. GROund disconnects all sources without turning AM off. *RST value is DC.

commands.name: fm_deviation
commands.description: Set/query FM deviation. *RST value is 1 kHz.

commands.name: fm_deviation_step
commands.description: Set/query the FM deviation step size used by UP/DOWN. *RST value is 1 kHz.

commands.name: fm_state
commands.description: Turns FM ON or OFF. The instrument cannot do simultaneous FM and PM. *RST value is OFF.

commands.name: fm_source
commands.description: Selects FM source: INTernal, EXTernal, or INTernal,EXTernal. *RST value is INTernal.

commands.name: fm_coupling
commands.description: Set/query source coupling for FM. GROund disconnects all sources without turning FM off. *RST value is DC.

commands.name: fm_mode
commands.description: Selects true (LINear) or synthesized (DIGitized) FM (Special Function 120). *RST value is DIGitized.

commands.name: fm_delay
commands.description: Enables/disables the FM Delay Equalizer circuitry (Special Function 124). *RST value is ON.

commands.name: fm_impedance
commands.description: Returns the FM input impedance value (600 ohms).

commands.name: pm_deviation
commands.description: Set/query phase modulation (PM, displayed as PhiM) deviation. Cannot run simultaneously with FM. Deviation is set in degrees or radians.

commands.name: pm_state
commands.description: Turns PM ON or OFF. Turning PM on while FM is on turns FM off. *RST value is OFF.

commands.name: pm_source
commands.description: Selects PM source: INTernal, EXTernal, or INTernal,EXTernal. *RST value is INTernal.

commands.name: pm_coupling
commands.description: Set/query source coupling for PM. GROund disconnects all sources without turning PM off. *RST value is DC.

commands.name: pulse_state
commands.description: Turns pulse modulation ON or OFF (requires Option 008). *RST value is OFF.

commands.name: pulse_source
commands.description: Selects the pulse source: INTernal or EXTernal (de-coupled only). *RST value is EXTernal.

commands.name: pulse_impedance
commands.description: Set/query the input impedance of the PULSE connector. May be set to 50 ohms (Special Function 210). *RST value is 100 kohms.

commands.name: pulse_conditioning
commands.description: Selects pulse control type (Special Function 211). OFF = direct pulse control; ON = internal pulse generator. *RST value is OFF (direct).

commands.name: pulse_delay
commands.description: Set/query the pulse delay (Special Function 212). *RST value is 1 us.

commands.name: pulse_delay_step
commands.description: Set/query the pulse delay step size used by UP/DOWN. *RST value is 1 us.

commands.name: pulse_width
commands.description: Set/query the pulse width (Special Function 213). *RST value is 1 us.

commands.name: pulse_width_step
commands.description: Set/query the pulse width step size used by UP/DOWN. *RST value is 1 us.

commands.name: pulse_slope
commands.description: Set/query the pulse triggering edge (Special Function 214). *RST value is POSitive.

commands.name: pulse_frequency
commands.description: Set/query the pulse rate frequency. Alias to LFSource:FREQuency.

commands.name: lfsource_frequency
commands.description: Set/query the internal audio (LF) source frequency. Equivalent to <mod_type>:FREQ. *RST value is 1 kHz.

commands.name: lfsource_frequency_step
commands.description: Set/query the LF source frequency step size used by UP/DOWN. *RST value is 100 Hz.

commands.name: lfsource_state
commands.description: Turns the LF source ON or OFF. Cannot be turned off while a modulation type using the internal source is on. *RST value is OFF.

commands.name: lfsource_waveform
commands.description: Selects the LF source waveform (Special Function 130). *RST value is SINe.

parameters.name: waveform
parameters.range: SINe | SQUare | TRIangle | SAWTooth | WGNoise (White Gaussian Noise)

commands.name: lfsource_level
commands.description: Set/query the LF (audio) source output level in volts. *RST value is 1 V.

commands.name: lfsource_level_step
commands.description: Set/query the LF source level step size used by UP/DOWN. *RST value is 0.1 V.

commands.name: lfsource_trigger
commands.description: Causes a one-shot trigger of the LF source if its SOURce is EXTernal (Special Function 132 with 131 on).

commands.name: lfsource_trigger_source
commands.description: Defines whether the LF source is CONTinuous or EXTernal (triggered by an external transition) (Special Function 131). *RST value is CONTinuous.

commands.name: lfsource_frequency2
commands.description: Set/query the frequency of the Channel 2 audio source (Special Function 133 on). Short form FREQ2. *RST value is 400 Hz.

commands.name: lfsource_waveform2
commands.description: Selects the Channel 2 audio source waveform (Special Function 135). Short form WAV2. *RST value is SINe.

commands.name: lfsource_state2
commands.description: Turns the Channel 2 audio source ON or OFF (Special Function 133). Short form STAT2. *RST value is OFF.

commands.name: lfsource_level2
commands.description: Set/query the level of the Channel 2 audio source (Special Function 134). Short form LEV2. *RST value is 100 mV.

commands.name: lfsource_phase2
commands.description: Adjusts the phase of the Channel 2 audio source in degrees or radians (Special Function 136). Short form PHAS2. *RST value is 0 deg.

commands.name: marker_frequency
commands.description: Set/query the frequency of the selected marker (MARKer/MARKer1, MARKer2, MARKer3). Markers use FREQ:OFFSet and FREQ:MULT. *RST value is 100,000.00 Hz.

commands.name: marker_state
commands.description: Turns the specified marker ON or OFF (not turned on by setting marker frequency). *RST value is OFF.

commands.name: marker_all_off
commands.description: Turns off all markers (the *RST condition). Accepted on any specific marker but always turns off all markers. Cannot be queried.

commands.name: phase_adjust
commands.description: Controls the RF output phase offset relative to the reference (Special Function 110). Base unit is radians; queries return radians. *RST value is 0.

commands.name: phase_step
commands.description: Set/query the phase step size used by UP/DOWN. *RST value is one degree.

commands.name: phase_reference
commands.description: Resets the PHASe value to 0 without changing the actual phase. Subsequent phase references are relative to this point.

commands.name: reference_oscillator_calibration
commands.description: Adjusts the internal 10 MHz reference oscillator frequency (Special Function 160). Range 0-255; a change of 1 corresponds to about 4 Hz. Returns to calibrated value at *RST.

commands.name: reference_oscillator_source
commands.description: Queries the current reference source (INT or EXT) (Special Function 161).

commands.name: reference_oscillator_auto
commands.description: Set/query whether an external timebase is allowed (Special Function 162). ON: internal or external timebase may be used; OFF: only internal. *RST value is ON.

commands.name: sequence_register
commands.description: Sets up a list of save/recall registers (0-9, max 10) to auto-sequence through (front-panel SET SEQ). Sending the null list clears the sequence. Any HP-IB command aborts the auto-sequence.

commands.name: sequence_immediate
commands.description: Sequences to the next register in the register list.

commands.name: sequence_state
commands.description: When ON, the Auto Sequence state is active (front-panel AUTO SEQ): the instrument automatically steps through the register list (1 s per register, or sweep duration for a sweep). *RST value is OFF.

commands.name: sweep_time
commands.description: Set/query the sweep time. UP/DOWN step to the next/previous valid 1-2-5 setting. Does not turn sweep on (use FREQ:MODE SWEep or INIT). *RST value is 1 second.

commands.name: sweep_mode
commands.description: Selects sweep type. AUTO allows single or continuous sweeps; MANual allows frequency control via FREQuency:MANual. *RST value is AUTO.

commands.name: sweep_spacing
commands.description: Selects LINear or LOGarithmic sweep spacing. *RST value is LINear.

commands.name: sweep_generation
commands.description: Selects STEPped (digitally stepped) or ANALog (phase-continuous) sweep (Special Function 112). *RST value is STEPped.

commands.name: take_sweep
commands.description: Stops any sweep and takes a single sweep. Equivalent to INIT:ABORt; SWE:MODE AUTO; FREQ:MODE SWEep; INIT:MODE SINgle; INIT:IMMediate.

commands.name: initialize_immediate
commands.description: Sets INITialize:STATe to RUN and starts a single or continuous sweep. If a sweep is already in progress, it is aborted and restarted.

commands.name: initialize_abort
commands.description: Aborts any current sweep and sets INITialize:STATe to PAUSe.

commands.name: initialize_state
commands.description: Returns PAUSe or RUN, indicating whether the instrument is sweeping or idle (meaningful only when FREQ:MODE is SWEep). *RST value is PAUSe.

commands.name: initialize_mode
commands.description: Determines single (SINGle) or continuous (CONTinuous) sweep. After a single sweep, INITialize:STATe becomes PAUSe and an INIT is required to restart. *RST value is CONTinuous.

commands.name: modulation_state
commands.description: Toggles on/off the previously selected modulation type. STATe OFF turns off all modulation and sets LFSource:STATe OFF. Query returns 1 if any modulation is on, else 0. *RST: previously-active modulation list is FM.

commands.name: display_state
commands.description: Blanks or enables the front-panel display.

commands.name: display_annotation_all
commands.description: Enables/disables the front-panel display annotation (Special Function 191). *RST value is ON.

commands.name: display_annotation_frequency
commands.description: Enables/disables front-panel display of RF output frequency (Special Function 192). *RST value is ON.

commands.name: display_annotation_modulation
commands.description: Enables/disables front-panel display of modulation (Special Function 193). *RST value is ON.

commands.name: display_annotation_amplitude
commands.description: Enables/disables front-panel display of amplitude (Special Function 195). *RST value is ON.

commands.name: display_annotation_lfsource
commands.description: Enables/disables front-panel display of the audio source (Special Function 194). *RST value is ON.

commands.name: display_radix
commands.description: Selects US or EURopean radix for front-panel numbers (Special Function 196). Affects front-panel display only; HP-IB numbers must use US radix. *RST value is US.

commands.name: system_error
commands.description: Reads an error from the system error queue (returns 0 if empty). NUMeric returns the error number only; STRing returns number and a quoted message. See manual Appendix D for the full error list.

commands.name: system_state_clear
commands.description: Clears all save/recall registers.

commands.name: system_security
commands.description: Controls the security mode (Special Function 173). When secure, disabled display annunciators cannot be re-enabled. Switching ON to OFF erases all memory (RAM wipe). Not affected by *RST or *RCL.

commands.name: status_device_event
commands.description: Queries the Device Dependent Event Status Register.

commands.name: status_device_condition
commands.description: Queries the Device Dependent Condition Status Register.

commands.name: status_device_enable
commands.description: Set/query the Device Dependent Event Enable Register.

commands.name: status_questionable_event
commands.description: Queries the HP-SL Signal Integrity Event Status Register.

commands.name: status_questionable_condition
commands.description: Queries the HP-SL Signal Integrity Condition Status Register.

commands.name: status_questionable_enable
commands.description: Set/query the HP-SL Signal Integrity Event Enable Register.

commands.name: power_meter
commands.description: Queries the internal power meter (Special Function 182). The power meter connector is inside the instrument under the top cover.

commands.name: voltmeter_voltage
commands.description: Measures the voltage at the rear-panel VM IN (voltmeter input) connector using the internal voltmeter.

commands.name: voltmeter_mode
commands.description: Selects DC or AC (rms) voltmeter measurement (Special Functions 180/181). *RST is DC.
